title: Bitwise XOR value isolation from packed slot configuration
explanation: When an address and a smaller integer value are packed tightly into a single 256-bit storage slot, developers traditionally extract the integer using a standard bitmask constraint. However, if the address has already been extracted via right shifting (`shr(96, packed)`), executing an `xor` operation against the left-shifted address (`shl(96, receiver)`) cancels out the matching upper bits entirely, yielding the remaining lower bits directly. This saves gas by replacing expensive bitmask constants with quick register operations.
when_to_apply: Unpacking a lower-bit integer field from a storage layout word where the upper-bit address has already been extracted into memory.
when_not_to_apply: When the upper bits have not yet been explicitly isolated or if dirty data bits exist within the address variable range.
